_comment: exp_016 — best-of-N self-consistency on top of exp_014. N=3 stochastic samples per candidate, judger-equivalence clustering, >=2/3 majority vote, fall back to exp_014's answer on no-majority. Same GRPO model as exp_014 stage 1 — no prompt change, no training. Public eval uses wrong-cases; private submission uses --mode full or --mode missing (see notes.md).
_dataset_note: Kaggle dataset holding exp_014's public_responses.jsonl + private_responses.jsonl. Re-upload if exp_014 outputs change.

model._model_id_note: Same model as exp_014 stage 1 — best-of-N is sampling-time diversity, not a different policy.

sampling._temperature_note: T=1.0 matches exp_009 GRPO training temperature. T=0.9 risks staying in the wrong argmax basin.
sampling._max_tokens_note: exp_014 missing_boxed cases are all truncation under GRPO at 4096. Bump to 8192 (inside max_model_len=10240) so the chain actually closes.

voting._voting_note: Cluster the 3 boxed answers by Judger.auto_judge pairwise equivalence (so 325*326 ≡ 105950 counts as agreement). Pick the largest cluster; replace exp_014's answer only if it has >=2 members. No-majority → keep exp_014's answer. This is the no-regression safety net.

selection._public_mode_note: On public.jsonl, candidate set = cases exp_014 got wrong (329 wrong_ff_boxed + 78 missing_boxed + 103 wrong_mcq).
selection._private_mode_note: On private.jsonl, candidate set = ALL 943 questions (we have no ground truth). Switch to 'missing' for the ~78 missing_boxed-only quick run.
